ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "asin" TEXT /* ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) of the product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Current price of the product */,
  "launch_date" TEXT /* Date when the product was launched */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviewers" (
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reviewer */,
  "reviewer_name" TEXT /* Name of the reviewer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the reviewer */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the reviewer joined the platform */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the reviewer */,
  "review_count" INTEGER /* Total number of reviews posted by the reviewer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reviewer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given (1-5) */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of reviews with this rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result */,
  "review_id" INTEGER /* ID of the review */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment of the review (positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Numerical score representing the sentiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytical result */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., average rating, review count) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the analytical data was recorded */,
  "period" TEXT /* Time period for which the analytics is calculated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)
column_names: ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) of the product
Date when the product was launched
Date when the reviewer joined the platform
Total number of reviews posted by the reviewer
Number of reviews with this rating
Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result
Sentiment of the review (positive, negative, neutral)
Unique identifier for each analytical result
Name of the metric (e.g., average rating, review count)
Date when the analytical data was recorded
Time period for which the analytics is calculated
